expectations.text: Defines prior with correct support for all 4 parameters (beta>0, gamma>0, 0<i0<1, 0<p<1)
expectations.evidence: Lines 54-59: beta ~ Gamma(2, 0.3) (positive), gamma ~ Gamma(2, 0.15) (positive), i0 ~ Beta(2, 50) (0,1), p ~ Beta(5, 2) (0,1). All supports are correct.

expectations.text: Uses bf.Adapter() with explicit constraint handling
expectations.evidence: Lines 118-132: `adapter = bf.Adapter()` with `.constrain()` calls for all 4 parameters.

expectations.text: Calls .constrain() with lower=0 for beta and gamma
Calls .constrain() with lower=0, upper=1 for i0 and p
expectations.evidence: Line 125: `.constrain("i0", lower=0, upper=1)` and Line 126: `.constrain("p", lower=0, upper=1)`.

expectations.text: Routes the daily case counts through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 132: `.concatenate(["daily_cases"], into="summary_variables")`. No inference_conditions routing present.

expectations.text: Uses a summary network appropriate for sequential data (TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or SetTransformer if treating days as exchangeable is justified and documented)
expectations.evidence: Lines 97-105: `bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer(...)` used. Analysis notes (lines 19-20) explicitly justify: 'Daily case counts over 30 days are an ordered sequence -- day order matters for epidemic dynamics. A SetTransformer would destroy temporal structure.'

expectations.text: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
expectations.evidence: Lines 94-109: Comments state 'Small config'. TimeSeriesTransformer uses embed_dims=(32,32), num_heads=(2,2), mlp_depths=(1,1), mlp_widths=(64,64). FlowMatching uses subnet widths (128,128). Analysis notes line 25-26 confirm: 'Started with Small config per the skill's hard rule.'

expectations.text: Sets summary_dim to approximately 2x the number of parameters (around 8)
Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Runs in-silico diagnostics before any real-data inference
expectations.evidence: Lines 196-226: In-silico diagnostics run with 300 simulated datasets via `workflow.simulate(300)`, `workflow.plot_default_diagnostics()`, `workflow.compute_default_diagnostics()`, and `check_diagnostics()`. Real-data inference (section 8, lines 233-267) is commented out and comes after.

expectations.text: Checks all 4 parameters individually against house thresholds
expectations.evidence: Lines 208-215: `check_diagnostics(metrics)` is called on the metrics DataFrame which contains per-parameter diagnostics. Lines 218-222 enforce STOP/WARN decisions. Analysis notes line 60 confirms: 'Hard stop if any parameter exceeds ECE > 0.10 or NRMSE > 0.15'.

expectations.text: Does NOT flatten the time series into inference_conditions
expectations.evidence: Lines 118-132: The adapter routes daily_cases via `.as_time_series(["daily_cases"])` (line 121) then `.concatenate(["daily_cases"], into="summary_variables")` (line 132). There is no mention of inference_conditions anywhere in the code.
